CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3079-* Documented NodePort networking for CDK. ([#44863](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44863), [@chuckbutler](https://github.com/chuckbutler))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3080-* Deployments and DaemonSets are now considered complete once all of the new pods are up and running - affects `kubectl rollout status` (and ProgressDeadlineSeconds for Deployments) ([#44672](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44672), [@kargakis](https://github.com/kargakis))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3081-* Exclude nodes labeled as master from LoadBalancer / NodePort; restores documented behaviour. ([#44745](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44745), [@justinsb](https://github.com/justinsb))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3082-* Fixes issue during LB creation where ports where incorrectly assigned to a floating IP ([#44387](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44387), [@jamiehannaford](https://github.com/jamiehannaford))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3083-* Remove redis-proxy.yaml sample, as the image is nowhere to be found. ([#44801](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44801), [@klausenbusk](https://github.com/klausenbusk))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md:3084:* Resolves juju vsphere hostname bug showing only a single node in a scaled node-pool. ([#44780](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44780), [@chuckbutler](https://github.com/chuckbutler))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3085-* kubectl commands run inside a pod using a kubeconfig file now use the namespace specified in the kubeconfig file, instead of using the pod namespace. If no kubeconfig file is used, or the kubeconfig does not specify a namespace, the pod namespace is still used as a fallback. ([#44570](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44570), [@liggitt](https://github.com/liggitt))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3086-* This adds support for CNI ConfigLists, which permit plugin chaining. ([#42202](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/42202), [@squeed](https://github.com/squeed))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3087-* API requests using impersonation now include the `system:authenticated` group in the impersonated user automatically. ([#44076](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44076), [@liggitt](https://github.com/liggitt))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3088-* Print conditions of RC/RS in 'kubectl describe' command. ([#44710](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44710), [@xiangpengzhao](https://github.com/xiangpengzhao))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3089-* cinder: Add support for the KVM virtio-scsi driver ([#41498](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/41498), [@mikebryant](https://github.com/mikebryant))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3090-* Disallows installation of upstream docker from PPA in the Juju kubernetes-worker charm. ([#44681](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44681), [@wwwtyro](https://github.com/wwwtyro))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3091-* Fluentd manifest pod is no longer created on non-registered master when creating clusters using kube-up.sh. ([#44721](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44721), [@piosz](https://github.com/piosz))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3092-* Job controller now respects ControllerRef to avoid fighting over Pods. ([#42176](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/42176), [@enisoc](https://github.com/enisoc))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3093-* CronJob controller now respects ControllerRef to avoid fighting with other controllers. ([#42177](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/42177), [@enisoc](https://github.com/enisoc))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md:3094:* The hyperkube image has been slimmed down and no longer includes addon manifests and other various scripts. These were introduced for the now removed docker-multinode setup system. ([#44555](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44555), [@luxas](https://github.com/luxas))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3095-* Refactoring reorganize taints function in kubectl to expose operations ([#43171](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/43171), [@ravisantoshgudimetla](https://github.com/ravisantoshgudimetla))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3096-* The Kubernetes API server now exits if it encounters a networking failure (e.g. the networking interface hosting its address goes away) to allow a process manager (systemd/kubelet/etc) to react to the problem.  Previously the server would log the failure and try again to bind to its configured address:port. ([#42272](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/42272), [@marun](https://github.com/marun))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3097-* Fixes a bug in the kubernetes-worker Juju charm code that attempted to give kube-proxy more than one api endpoint. ([#44677](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44677), [@wwwtyro](https://github.com/wwwtyro))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3098-* Fixes a missing comma in a list of strings. ([#44678](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44678), [@wwwtyro](https://github.com/wwwtyro))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3099-* Fix ceph-secret type to kubernetes.io/rbd in kubernetes-master charm ([#44635](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44635), [@Cynerva](https://github.com/Cynerva))
--
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.8.md-932-* scheduler: Fix issue were a new pod with affinity gets stuck at `creating` because the node had been deleted but the pod still exists. ([#56835](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/56835), [@wenlxie](https://github.com/wenlxie))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.8.md-933-* Updated Dashboard add-on to version 1.8.0: The Dashboard add-on now deploys with https enabled. The Dashboard can be accessed via kubectl proxy at http://localhost:8001/api/v1/namespaces/kube-system/services/https:kubernetes-dashboard:/proxy/. The /ui redirect is deprecated and will be removed in 1.10. ([#53046](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/53046), [@maciaszczykm](https://github.com/maciaszczykm))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.8.md-934-* Fix issue where masquerade rules are flushed in GCE k8s clusters. ([#56729](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/56729), [@dnardo](https://github.com/dnardo))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.8.md-935-* kubelet: Fix bug where `runAsUser: MustRunAsNonRoot` strategy didn't reject a pod with a non-numeric `USER`. ([#56708](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/56708), [@php-coder](https://github.com/php-coder))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.8.md-936-* Add iptables rules to allow Pod traffic even when default iptables policy is to reject. ([#52569](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/52569), [@tmjd](https://github.com/tmjd))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.8.md:937:* Fix a bug in GCE multizonal clusters where PersistentVolumes were sometimes created in zones without nodes. ([#52322](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/52322), [@davidz627](https://github.com/davidz627))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.8.md-938-* If a non-absolute mountPath is passed to the kubelet, prefix it with the appropriate root path. ([#55665](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/55665), [@brendandburns](https://github.com/brendandburns))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.8.md-939-* add GRS, RAGRS storage account type support for azure disk ([#55931](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/55931), [@andyzhangx](https://github.com/andyzhangx))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.8.md-940-* Fix a typo in prometheus-to-sd configuration, that drops some stackdriver metrics. ([#56473](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/56473), [@loburm](https://github.com/loburm))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.8.md-941-* Fixes server name verification of aggregated API servers and webhook admission endpoints ([#56415](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/56415), [@liggitt](https://github.com/liggitt))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.8.md-942-* Update jquery and bootstrap dependencies ([#56445](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/56445), [@dashpole](https://github.com/dashpole))

CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.9.md-1935-* A new field is added to CRI container log format to support splitting a long log line into multiple lines. ([#55922](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/55922), [@Random-Liu](https://github.com/Random-Liu))

/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.9.md-1939-    *  - User should be able to create k8s cluster which spans across multiple ESXi clusters, datacenters or even vCenters.
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.9.md-1940-    *  - vSphere cloud provider (VCP) uses OS hostname and not vSphere Inventory VM Name.
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.9.md-1941-    *    That means, now  VCP can handle cases where user changes VM inventory name.
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.9.md-1942-    * - VCP can handle cases where VM migrates to other ESXi cluster or datacenter or vCenter.

CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md-1120-```{"kind":"Status","apiVersion":"v1","metadata":{},"status":"Failure","message":"pods \"abc\" not found","reason":"NotFound","details":{"name":"abc","kind":"pods"},"code":404}```
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md-1121-In previous versions, the whole object was logged in audit events. Starting in 1.11, only `status`, `reason`, and `code` are logged. Code that relies on the older version must be updated to avoid errors.
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md-1122-([#62695](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/62695), [@CaoShuFeng](https://github.com/CaoShuFeng))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md-1123-* HTTP transport now uses `context.Context` to cancel dial operations. k8s.io/client-go/transport/Config struct has been updated to accept a function with a `context.Context` parameter. This is a breaking change if you use this field in your code. ([#60012](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/60012), [@ash2k](https://github.com/ash2k))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md-1124-* kubectl: This client version requires the `apps/v1` APIs, so it will not work against a cluster version older than v1.9.0. Note that kubectl only guarantees compatibility with clusters that are +/-1 minor version away. ([#61419](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/61419), [@enisoc](https://github.com/enisoc))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md:1125:[Omitted long matching line]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md-1126-* Default mount propagation has changed from `HostToContainer` ("rslave" in Linux terminology), as it was in 1.10, to `None` ("private") to match the behavior in 1.9 and earlier releases; `HostToContainer` as a default caused regressions in some pods. If you are relying on this behavior you will need to set it explicitly. ([#62462](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/62462), [@jsafrane](https://github.com/jsafrane))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md-1127-* The kube-apiserver `--storage-version` flag has been removed; you must use `--storage-versions` instead. ([#61453](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/61453), [@hzxuzhonghu](https://github.com/hzxuzhonghu))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md-1128-* Authors of aggregated API servers must not rely on authorization being done by the kube-apiserver, and must do delegated authorization in addition. ([#61349](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/61349), [@sttts](https://github.com/sttts))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md-1129-* GC is now bound by QPS so if you need more QPS to avoid ratelimiting GC, you'll have to set it explicitly. ([#63657](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/63657), [@shyamjvs](https://github.com/shyamjvs))
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md-1130-[Omitted long context line]

CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.15.md-216-- Fixed a data race in kubelet image manager that can cause static pod workers to silently stop working. ([#88915](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/88915), [@roycaihw](https://github.com/roycaihw)) [SIG Node]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.15.md-217-- Fixed an issue that could cause the kubelet to incorrectly run concurrent pod reconciliation loops and crash. ([#89055](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/89055), [@tedyu](https://github.com/tedyu)) [SIG Node]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.15.md-218-- Fixes conversion error for HorizontalPodAutoscaler objects with invalid annotations ([#89969](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/89969), [@liggitt](https://github.com/liggitt)) [SIG Autoscaling]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.15.md-219-- Fixes conversion error in multi-version custom resources that could cause metadata.generation to increment on no-op patches or updates of a custom resource. ([#88995](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/88995), [@liggitt](https://github.com/liggitt)) [SIG API Machinery]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.15.md-220-- For GCE cluster provider, fix bug of not being able to create internal type load balancer for clusters with more than 1000 nodes in a single zone. ([#89902](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/89902), [@wojtek-t](https://github.com/wojtek-t)) [SIG Cloud Provider, Network and Scalability]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.15.md:221:- For volumes that allow attaches across multiple nodes, attach and detach operations across different nodes are now executed in parallel. ([#89241](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/89241), [@verult](https://github.com/verult)) [SIG Apps, Node and Storage]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.15.md-222-- Kubelet metrics gathered through metrics-server or prometheus should no longer timeout for Windows nodes running more than 3 pods. /workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1930-    
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1931-- Kubeadm: improve the validation of serviceSubnet and podSubnet.
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1932-  ServiceSubnet has to be limited in size, due to implementation details, and the mask can not allocate more than 20 bits.
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1933-  PodSubnet validates against the corresponding cluster "--node-cidr-mask-size" of the kube-controller-manager, it fail if the values are not compatible.
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1934-  kubeadm no longer sets the node-mask automatically on IPv6 deployments, you must check that your IPv6 service subnet mask is compatible with the default node mask /64 or set it accordingly.
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md:1935:  Previously, for IPv6, if the podSubnet had a mask lower than /112, kubeadm calculated a node-mask to be multiple of eight and splitting the available bits to maximise the number used for nodes. ([#95723](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/95723), [@aojea](https://github.com/aojea)) [SIG Cluster Lifecycle]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1936-- The deprecated flag --experimental-kustomize is now removed from kubeadm commands. Use --experimental-patches instead, which was introduced in 1.19. Migration information available in --help description for --experimental-patches. ([#94871](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/94871), [@neolit123](https://github.com/neolit123))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1937-- Windows hyper-v container feature gate is deprecated in 1.20 and will be removed in 1.21 ([#95505](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/95505), [@wawa0210](https://github.com/wawa0210)) [SIG Node and Windows]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1938-- The kube-apiserver ability to serve on an insecure port, deprecated since v1.10, has been removed. The insecure address flags `--address` and `--insecure-bind-address` have no effect in kube-apiserver and will be removed in v1.24. The insecure port flags `--port` and `--insecure-port` may only be set to 0 and will be removed in v1.24. ([#95856](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/95856), [@knight42](https://github.com/knight42), [SIG API Machinery, Node, Testing])
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1939-- Add dual-stack Services (alpha).  This is a BREAKING CHANGE to an alpha API.
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1940-  It changes the dual-stack API wrt Service from a single ipFamily field to 3
--
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-3051-
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-3052- - Kubeadm: improve the validation of serviceSubnet and podSubnet.
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-3053-  ServiceSubnet has to be limited in size, due to implementation details, and the mask can not allocate more than 20 bits.
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-3054-  PodSubnet validates against the corresponding cluster "--node-cidr-mask-size" of the kube-controller-manager, it fail if the values are not compatible.
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-3055-  kubeadm no longer sets the node-mask automatically on IPv6 deployments, you must check that your IPv6 service subnet mask is compatible with the default node mask /64 or set it accordingly.
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md:3056:  Previously, for IPv6, if the podSubnet had a mask lower than /112, kubeadm calculated a node-mask to be multiple of eight and splitting the available bits to maximise the number used for nodes. ([#95723](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/95723), [@aojea](https://github.com/aojea)) [SIG Cluster Lifecycle]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-3057-  - Windows hyper-v container feature gate is deprecated in 1.20 and will be removed in 1.21 ([#95505](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/95505), [@wawa0210](https://github.com/wawa0210)) [SIG Node and Windows]

/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.21.md-1789-  vSphere releases less than 67u3 are deprecated as of v1.21. Please consider upgrading vSphere to 67u3 or above. vSphere CSI Driver requires minimum vSphere 67u3.
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.21.md-1790-  
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.21.md-1791-  VM Hardware version less than 15 is deprecated as of v1.21. Please consider upgrading the Node VM Hardware version to 15 or above. vSphere CSI Driver recommends Node VM's Hardware version set to at least vmx-15.
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.21.md-1792-  
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.21.md:1793:  Multi vCenter support is deprecated as of v1.21. CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-2504-   `JobTrackingWithFinalizers` is still disabled by default. ([#109486](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/109486), [@alculquicondor](https://github.com/alculquicondor))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-2505-- Fixed `NeedResize` build failure on Windows. ([#109721](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/109721), [@andyzhangx](https://github.com/andyzhangx))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-2506-- Fixed a bug in `kubectl` that caused the wrong result length when using `--chunk-size` and `--selector` together. ([#110652](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110652), [@Abirdcfly](https://github.com/Abirdcfly))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md:2507:- Fixed a bug involving Services of type `LoadBalancer` with multiple IPs and a `LoadBalancerSourceRanges` that overlaps the node IP. ([#109826](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/109826), [@danwinship](https://github.com/danwinship))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-2508-- Fixed a bug which could have allowed an improperly annotated LoadBalancer service to become active. ([#109601](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/109601), [@mdbooth](https://github.com/mdbooth))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-2509-- Fixed a kubelet issue that could result in invalid pod status updates to be sent to the api-server where pods would be reported in a terminal phase but also report a ready condition of true in some cases. ([#110256](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110256), [@bobbypage](https://github.com/bobbypage))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-2510-- Fixed an issue on Windows nodes where `HostProcess` containers may not be created as expected. ([#110140](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110140), [@marosset](https://github.com/marosset))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-2511-- Fixed bug where CSI migration doesn't count inline volumes for attach limit. ([#107787](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/107787), [@Jiawei0227](https://github.com/Jiawei0227))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-2512-- Fixed error "dbus: connection closed by user" after dbus daemon restarts. ([#110496](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110496), [@kolyshkin](https://github.com/kolyshkin))
--
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-3561-- Fix printing resources with int64 fields ([#110408](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110408), [@tkashem](https://github.com/tkashem)) [SIG API Machinery]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-3562-- Fix spurious kube-apiserver log warnings related to openapi v3 merging when creating or modifying CustomResourceDefinition objects ([#109880](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/109880), [@Jefftree](https://github.com/Jefftree)) [SIG API Machinery and Testing]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-3563-- Fix the bug that a ServiceIPStaticSubrange enabled cluster assigns duplicate IP addresses when the dynamic block is exhausted. ([#109928](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/109928), [@tksm](https://github.com/tksm)) [SIG Network]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-3564-- Fix the bug that the metrics for the cluster IP allocator are incorrectly reported. ([#110027](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110027), [@tksm](https://github.com/tksm)) [SIG Instrumentation]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-3565-- Fixed a kubelet issue that could result in invalid pod status updates to be sent to the api-server where pods would be reported in a terminal phase but also report a ready condition of true in some cases. ([#110256](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110256), [@bobbypage](https://github.com/bobbypage)) [SIG Node and Testing]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md:3566:- Fixed a long-standing but very obscure bug involving Services of type LoadBalancer with multiple IPs and a LoadBalancerSourceRanges that overlaps the node IP. ([#109826](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/109826), [@danwinship](https://github.com/danwinship)) [SIG Network]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-3567-- Fixes strict server-side field validation treating metadata fields as unknown fields ([#109268](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/109268), [@liggitt](https://github.com/liggitt)) [SIG API Machinery and Testing]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-3568-- Kube-apiserver: Get, GetList and Watch requests that should be served by the apiserver cacher during shutdown will be rejected to avoid a deadlock situation leaving requests hanging. ([#108414](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/108414), [@aojea](https://github.com/aojea)) [SIG API Machinery]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-3569-- Kubeadm: only taint control plane nodes when the legacy "master" taint is present. This avoids a bug where "kubeadm upgrade" will re-taint a control plane node with the new "control plane" taint even if the user explicitly untainted the node. ([#109840](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/109840), [@neolit123](https://github.com/neolit123)) [SIG Cluster Lifecycle]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-3570-- Kubeadm: pass the host OS environment variables when executing "crictl" during image pulls. This fixes a bug where *PROXY environment variables did not affect crictl's internet connectivity. ([#110134](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110134), [@mk46](https://github.com/mk46)) [SIG Cluster Lifecycle]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-3571-- Kubelet: wait for node allocatable ephemeral-storage data ([#101882](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/101882), [@jackfrancis](https://github.com/jackfrancis)) [SIG Node and Storage]
--

CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3079-* Documented NodePort networking for CDK. ([#44863](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44863), [@chuckbutler](https://github.com/chuckbutler))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3080-* Deployments and DaemonSets are now considered complete once all of the new pods are up and running - affects `kubectl rollout status` (and ProgressDeadlineSeconds for Deployments) ([#44672](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44672), [@kargakis](https://github.com/kargakis))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3081-* Exclude nodes labeled as master from LoadBalancer / NodePort; restores documented behaviour. ([#44745](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44745), [@justinsb](https://github.com/justinsb))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3082-* Fixes issue during LB creation where ports where incorrectly assigned to a floating IP ([#44387](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44387), [@jamiehannaford](https://github.com/jamiehannaford))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3083-* Remove redis-proxy.yaml sample, as the image is nowhere to be found. ([#44801](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44801), [@klausenbusk](https://github.com/klausenbusk))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md:3084:* Resolves juju vsphere hostname bug showing only a single node in a scaled node-pool. ([#44780](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44780), [@chuckbutler](https://github.com/chuckbutler))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3085-* kubectl commands run inside a pod using a kubeconfig file now use the namespace specified in the kubeconfig file, instead of using the pod namespace. If no kubeconfig file is used, or the kubeconfig does not specify a namespace, the pod namespace is still used as a fallback. ([#44570](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44570), [@liggitt](https://github.com/liggitt))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3086-* This adds support for CNI ConfigLists, which permit plugin chaining. ([#42202](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/42202), [@squeed](https://github.com/squeed))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3087-* API requests using impersonation now include the `system:authenticated` group in the impersonated user automatically. ([#44076](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44076), [@liggitt](https://github.com/liggitt))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3088-* Print conditions of RC/RS in 'kubectl describe' command. ([#44710](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44710), [@xiangpengzhao](https://github.com/xiangpengzhao))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3089-* cinder: Add support for the KVM virtio-scsi driver ([#41498](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/41498), [@mikebryant](https://github.com/mikebryant))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3090-* Disallows installation of upstream docker from PPA in the Juju kubernetes-worker charm. ([#44681](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44681), [@wwwtyro](https://github.com/wwwtyro))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3091-* Fluentd manifest pod is no longer created on non-registered master when creating clusters using kube-up.sh. ([#44721](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44721), [@piosz](https://github.com/piosz))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3092-* Job controller now respects ControllerRef to avoid fighting over Pods. ([#42176](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/42176), [@enisoc](https://github.com/enisoc))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3093-* CronJob controller now respects ControllerRef to avoid fighting with other controllers. ([#42177](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/42177), [@enisoc](https://github.com/enisoc))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md:3094:* The hyperkube image has been slimmed down and no longer includes addon manifests and other various scripts. These were introduced for the now removed docker-multinode setup system. ([#44555](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44555), [@luxas](https://github.com/luxas))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3095-* Refactoring reorganize taints function in kubectl to expose operations ([#43171](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/43171), [@ravisantoshgudimetla](https://github.com/ravisantoshgudimetla))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3096-* The Kubernetes API server now exits if it encounters a networking failure (e.g. the networking interface hosting its address goes away) to allow a process manager (systemd/kubelet/etc) to react to the problem.  Previously the server would log the failure and try again to bind to its configured address:port. ([#42272](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/42272), [@marun](https://github.com/marun))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3097-* Fixes a bug in the kubernetes-worker Juju charm code that attempted to give kube-proxy more than one api endpoint. ([#44677](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44677), [@wwwtyro](https://github.com/wwwtyro))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3098-* Fixes a missing comma in a list of strings. ([#44678](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44678), [@wwwtyro](https://github.com/wwwtyro))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.7.md-3099-* Fix ceph-secret type to kubernetes.io/rbd in kubernetes-master charm ([#44635](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/44635), [@Cynerva](https://github.com/Cynerva))

CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.8.md-932-* scheduler: Fix issue were a new pod with affinity gets stuck at `creating` because the node had been deleted but the pod still exists. ([#56835](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/56835), [@wenlxie](https://github.com/wenlxie))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.8.md-933-* Updated Dashboard add-on to version 1.8.0: The Dashboard add-on now deploys with https enabled. The Dashboard can be accessed via kubectl proxy at http://localhost:8001/api/v1/namespaces/kube-system/services/https:kubernetes-dashboard:/proxy/. The /ui redirect is deprecated and will be removed in 1.10. ([#53046](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/53046), [@maciaszczykm](https://github.com/maciaszczykm))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.8.md-934-* Fix issue where masquerade rules are flushed in GCE k8s clusters. ([#56729](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/56729), [@dnardo](https://github.com/dnardo))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.8.md-935-* kubelet: Fix bug where `runAsUser: MustRunAsNonRoot` strategy didn't reject a pod with a non-numeric `USER`. ([#56708](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/56708), [@php-coder](https://github.com/php-coder))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.8.md-936-* Add iptables rules to allow Pod traffic even when default iptables policy is to reject. ([#52569](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/52569), [@tmjd](https://github.com/tmjd))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.8.md:937:* Fix a bug in GCE multizonal clusters where PersistentVolumes were sometimes created in zones without nodes. ([#52322](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/52322), [@davidz627](https://github.com/davidz627))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.8.md-938-* If a non-absolute mountPath is passed to the kubelet, prefix it with the appropriate root path. ([#55665](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/55665), [@brendandburns](https://github.com/brendandburns))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.8.md-939-* add GRS, RAGRS storage account type support for azure disk ([#55931](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/55931), [@andyzhangx](https://github.com/andyzhangx))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.8.md-940-* Fix a typo in prometheus-to-sd configuration, that drops some stackdriver metrics. ([#56473](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/56473), [@loburm](https://github.com/loburm))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.8.md-941-* Fixes server name verification of aggregated API servers and webhook admission endpoints ([#56415](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/56415), [@liggitt](https://github.com/liggitt))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.8.md-942-* Update jquery and bootstrap dependencies ([#56445](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/56445), [@dashpole](https://github.com/dashpole))

CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.9.md-1935-* A new field is added to CRI container log format to support splitting a long log line into multiple lines. ([#55922](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/55922), [@Random-Liu](https://github.com/Random-Liu))

CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md-1120-```{"kind":"Status","apiVersion":"v1","metadata":{},"status":"Failure","message":"pods \"abc\" not found","reason":"NotFound","details":{"name":"abc","kind":"pods"},"code":404}```
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md-1121-In previous versions, the whole object was logged in audit events. Starting in 1.11, only `status`, `reason`, and `code` are logged. Code that relies on the older version must be updated to avoid errors.
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md-1122-([#62695](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/62695), [@CaoShuFeng](https://github.com/CaoShuFeng))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md-1123-* HTTP transport now uses `context.Context` to cancel dial operations. k8s.io/client-go/transport/Config struct has been updated to accept a function with a `context.Context` parameter. This is a breaking change if you use this field in your code. ([#60012](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/60012), [@ash2k](https://github.com/ash2k))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md-1124-* kubectl: This client version requires the `apps/v1` APIs, so it will not work against a cluster version older than v1.9.0. Note that kubectl only guarantees compatibility with clusters that are +/-1 minor version away. ([#61419](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/61419), [@enisoc](https://github.com/enisoc))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md:1125:[Omitted long matching line]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md-1126-* Default mount propagation has changed from `HostToContainer` ("rslave" in Linux terminology), as it was in 1.10, to `None` ("private") to match the behavior in 1.9 and earlier releases; `HostToContainer` as a default caused regressions in some pods. If you are relying on this behavior you will need to set it explicitly. ([#62462](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/62462), [@jsafrane](https://github.com/jsafrane))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md-1127-* The kube-apiserver `--storage-version` flag has been removed; you must use `--storage-versions` instead. ([#61453](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/61453), [@hzxuzhonghu](https://github.com/hzxuzhonghu))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md-1128-* Authors of aggregated API servers must not rely on authorization being done by the kube-apiserver, and must do delegated authorization in addition. ([#61349](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/61349), [@sttts](https://github.com/sttts))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.11.md-1129-* GC is now bound by QPS so if you need more QPS to avoid ratelimiting GC, you'll have to set it explicitly. ([#63657](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/63657), [@shyamjvs](https://github.com/shyamjvs))

CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.19.md-4443-- Fixes v1.18.0-rc.1 regression in `kubectl port-forward` when specifying a local and remote port ([#89401](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/89401), [@liggitt](https://github.com/liggitt)) [SIG CLI]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.19.md:4444:- For volumes that allow attaches across multiple nodes, attach and detach operations across different nodes are now executed in parallel. ([#88678](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/88678), [@verult](https://github.com/verult)) [SIG Apps, Node and Storage]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.19.md-4445-- Get-kube.sh uses the gcloud's current local GCP service account for auth when the provider is GCE or GKE instead of the metadata server default ([#88383](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/88383), [@BenTheElder](https://github.com/BenTheElder)) [SIG Cluster Lifecycle]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.19.md-4446-- Golang/x/net has been updated to bring in fixes for CVE-2020-9283 ([#88381](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/88381), [@BenTheElder](https://github.com/BenTheElder)) [SIG API Machinery, CLI, Cloud Provider, Cluster Lifecycle and Instrumentation]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.19.md-4447-- Hide kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration in describe command ([#88758](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/88758), [@soltysh](https://github.com/soltysh)) [SIG Auth and CLI]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.19.md-4448-- In GKE alpha clusters it will be possible to use the service annotation `cloud.google.com/network-tier: Standard` ([#88487](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/88487), [@zioproto](https://github.com/zioproto)) [SIG Cloud Provider]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.19.md-4449-- Ipvs: only attempt setting of sysctlconnreuse on supported kernels ([#88541](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/88541), [@cmluciano](https://github.com/cmluciano)) [SIG Network]

/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1930-    
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1931-- Kubeadm: improve the validation of serviceSubnet and podSubnet.
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1932-  ServiceSubnet has to be limited in size, due to implementation details, and the mask can not allocate more than 20 bits.
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1933-  PodSubnet validates against the corresponding cluster "--node-cidr-mask-size" of the kube-controller-manager, it fail if the values are not compatible.
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1934-  kubeadm no longer sets the node-mask automatically on IPv6 deployments, you must check that your IPv6 service subnet mask is compatible with the default node mask /64 or set it accordingly.
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md:1935:  Previously, for IPv6, if the podSubnet had a mask lower than /112, kubeadm calculated a node-mask to be multiple of eight and splitting the available bits to maximise the number used for nodes. ([#95723](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/95723), [@aojea](https://github.com/aojea)) [SIG Cluster Lifecycle]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1936-- The deprecated flag --experimental-kustomize is now removed from kubeadm commands. Use --experimental-patches instead, which was introduced in 1.19. Migration information available in --help description for --experimental-patches. ([#94871](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/94871), [@neolit123](https://github.com/neolit123))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1937-- Windows hyper-v container feature gate is deprecated in 1.20 and will be removed in 1.21 ([#95505](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/95505), [@wawa0210](https://github.com/wawa0210)) [SIG Node and Windows]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1938-- The kube-apiserver ability to serve on an insecure port, deprecated since v1.10, has been removed. The insecure address flags `--address` and `--insecure-bind-address` have no effect in kube-apiserver and will be removed in v1.24. The insecure port flags `--port` and `--insecure-port` may only be set to 0 and will be removed in v1.24. ([#95856](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/95856), [@knight42](https://github.com/knight42), [SIG API Machinery, Node, Testing])
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1939-- Add dual-stack Services (alpha).  This is a BREAKING CHANGE to an alpha API.
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.20.md-1940-  It changes the dual-stack API wrt Service from a single ipFamily field to 3
--

/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.21.md-1789-  vSphere releases less than 67u3 are deprecated as of v1.21. Please consider upgrading vSphere to 67u3 or above. vSphere CSI Driver requires minimum vSphere 67u3.
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.21.md-1790-  
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.21.md-1791-  VM Hardware version less than 15 is deprecated as of v1.21. Please consider upgrading the Node VM Hardware version to 15 or above. vSphere CSI Driver recommends Node VM's Hardware version set to at least vmx-15.
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.21.md-1792-  
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.21.md:1793:  Multi vCenter support is deprecated as of v1.21. If you have a Kubernetes cluster spanning across multiple vCenter servers, please consider moving all k8s nodes to a single vCenter Server. vSphere CSI Driver does not support Kubernetes deployment spanning across multiple vCenter servers.
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.21.md-1794-

/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-2502-  - was declaring a job finished before counting all the created pods in the status
/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-2503-  - was leaving pods with finalizers, blocking pod and job deletions
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-2504-   `JobTrackingWithFinalizers` is still disabled by default. ([#109486](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/109486), [@alculquicondor](https://github.com/alculquicondor))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-2505-- Fixed `NeedResize` build failure on Windows. ([#109721](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/109721), [@andyzhangx](https://github.com/andyzhangx))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-2506-- Fixed a bug in `kubectl` that caused the wrong result length when using `--chunk-size` and `--selector` together. ([#110652](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110652), [@Abirdcfly](https://github.com/Abirdcfly))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md:2507:- Fixed a bug involving Services of type `LoadBalancer` with multiple IPs and a `LoadBalancerSourceRanges` that overlaps the node IP. ([#109826](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/109826), [@danwinship](https://github.com/danwinship))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-2508-- Fixed a bug which could have allowed an improperly annotated LoadBalancer service to become active. ([#109601](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/109601), [@mdbooth](https://github.com/mdbooth))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-2509-- Fixed a kubelet issue that could result in invalid pod status updates to be sent to the api-server where pods would be reported in a terminal phase but also report a ready condition of true in some cases. ([#110256](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110256), [@bobbypage](https://github.com/bobbypage))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-2510-- Fixed an issue on Windows nodes where `HostProcess` containers may not be created as expected. ([#110140](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110140), [@marosset](https://github.com/marosset))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-2511-- Fixed bug where CSI migration doesn't count inline volumes for attach limit. ([#107787](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/107787), [@Jiawei0227](https://github.com/Jiawei0227))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-2512-- Fixed error "dbus: connection closed by user" after dbus daemon restarts. ([#110496](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110496), [@kolyshkin](https://github.com/kolyshkin))
--
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-3561-- Fix printing resources with int64 fields ([#110408](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110408), [@tkashem](https://github.com/tkashem)) [SIG API Machinery]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-3562-- Fix spurious kube-apiserver log warnings related to openapi v3 merging when creating or modifying CustomResourceDefinition objects ([#109880](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/109880), [@Jefftree](https://github.com/Jefftree)) [SIG API Machinery and Testing]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-3563-- Fix the bug that a ServiceIPStaticSubrange enabled cluster assigns duplicate IP addresses when the dynamic block is exhausted. ([#109928](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/109928), [@tksm](https://github.com/tksm)) [SIG Network]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-3564-- Fix the bug that the metrics for the cluster IP allocator are incorrectly reported. ([#110027](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110027), [@tksm](https://github.com/tksm)) [SIG Instrumentation]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-3565-- Fixed a kubelet issue that could result in invalid pod status updates to be sent to the api-server where pods would be reported in a terminal phase but also report a ready condition of true in some cases. ([#110256](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110256), [@bobbypage](https://github.com/bobbypage)) [SIG Node and Testing]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md:3566:- Fixed a long-standing but very obscure bug involving Services of type LoadBalancer with multiple IPs and a LoadBalancerSourceRanges that overlaps the node IP. ([#109826](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/109826), [@danwinship](https://github.com/danwinship)) [SIG Network]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-3567-- Fixes strict server-side field validation treating metadata fields as unknown fields ([#109268](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/109268), [@liggitt](https://github.com/liggitt)) [SIG API Machinery and Testing]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-3568-- Kube-apiserver: Get, GetList and Watch requests that should be served by the apiserver cacher during shutdown will be rejected to avoid a deadlock situation leaving requests hanging. ([#108414](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/108414), [@aojea](https://github.com/aojea)) [SIG API Machinery]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-3569-- Kubeadm: only taint control plane nodes when the legacy "master" taint is present. This avoids a bug where "kubeadm upgrade" will re-taint a control plane node with the new "control plane" taint even if the user explicitly untainted the node. ([#109840](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/109840), [@neolit123](https://github.com/neolit123)) [SIG Cluster Lifecycle]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-3570-- Kubeadm: pass the host OS environment variables when executing "crictl" during image pulls. This fixes a bug where *PROXY environment variables did not affect crictl's internet connectivity. ([#110134](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/110134), [@mk46](https://github.com/mk46)) [SIG Cluster Lifecycle]
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.25.md-3571-- Kubelet: wait for node allocatable ephemeral-storage data ([#101882](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/101882), [@jackfrancis](https://github.com/jackfrancis)) [SIG Node and Storage]
--

CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.9.md-2276-* "kubectl cp" updated to honor destination names  ([#51215](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/51215), [@juanvallejo](https://github.com/juanvallejo))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.9.md-2277-* kubeadm: Strip bootstrap tokens from the `kubeadm-config` ConfigMap ([#53559](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/53559), [@fabriziopandini](https://github.com/fabriziopandini))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.9.md-2278-* Skip podpreset test if the alpha feature settings/v1alpha1 is disabled ([#53080](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/53080), [@jennybuckley](https://github.com/jennybuckley))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.9.md-2279-* Log when node is successfully initialized by Cloud Controller Manager ([#53517](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/53517), [@andrewsykim](https://github.com/andrewsykim))
--
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.9.md-2625-* Delete load balancers if the UIDs for services don't match. ([#50539](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/50539), [@brendandburns](https://github.com/brendandburns))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.9.md-2626-* Show events when describing service accounts ([#51035](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/51035), [@mrogers950](https://github.com/mrogers950))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.9.md-2627-* implement proposal 34058: hostPath volume type ([#46597](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/46597), [@dixudx](https://github.com/dixudx))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.9.md-2628-* HostAlias is now supported for both non-HostNetwork Pods and HostNetwork Pods. ([#50646](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/50646), [@rickypai](https://github.com/rickypai))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.9.md-2629-* CRDs support metadata.generation and implement spec/status split ([#50764](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/50764), [@nikhita](https://github.com/nikhita))
CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.9.md:2630:* Allow attach of volumes to multiple nodes for vSphere ([#51066](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/51066), [@BaluDontu](https://github.com/BaluDontu))

results.content:
52:// ResourceSlice represents one or more resources in a pool of similar resources,
53:// managed by a common driver. A pool may span more than one ResourceSlice, and exactly how many
54:// ResourceSlices comprise a pool is determined by the driver.
57:// Each device in a given pool, regardless of how many ResourceSlices, must have a unique name.
59:// for a device is the tuple <driver name>, <pool name>, <device name>.
61:// Whenever a driver needs to update a pool, it increments the pool.Spec.Pool.Generation number
65:// When allocating all resources in a pool matching certain criteria or when
67:// consumer should check the number of ResourceSlices in a pool (included in
68:// each ResourceSlice) to determine whether its view of a pool is complete and
69:// if not, should wait until the driver has completed updating the pool.
110:	// Pool describes the pool that this ResourceSlice belongs to.
113:	Pool ResourcePool `json:"pool" protobuf:"bytes,2,name=pool"`
115:	// NodeName identifies the node which provides the resources in this pool.
119:	// This field can be used to limit access from nodes to ResourceSlices with
121:	// new nodes of the same type as some old node might also make new
124:	// Exactly one of NodeName, NodeSelector, AllNodes, and PerDeviceNodeSelection must be set.
131:	// NodeSelector defines which nodes have access to the resources in the pool,
132:	// when that pool is not limited to a single node.
136:	// Exactly one of NodeName, NodeSelector, AllNodes, and PerDeviceNodeSelection must be set.
142:	// AllNodes indicates that all nodes have access to the resources in the pool.
144:	// Exactly one of NodeName, NodeSelector, AllNodes, and PerDeviceNodeSelection must be set.
148:	AllNodes *bool `json:"allNodes,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,5,opt,name=allNodes"`
150:	// Devices lists some or all of the devices in this pool.
159:	// PerDeviceNodeSelection defines whether the access from nodes to
160:	// resources in the pool is set on the ResourceSlice level or on each
164:	// Exactly one of NodeName, NodeSelector, AllNodes, and PerDeviceNodeSelection must be set.
214:// ResourcePool describes the pool that ResourceSlices belong to.
216:	// Name is used to identify the pool. For node-local devices, this
225:	// Generation tracks the change in a pool over time. Whenever a driver
226:	// changes something about one or more of the resources in a pool, it
228:	// that pool. Consumers of ResourceSlices should only consider
229:	// resources from the pool with the highest generation number. The
231:	// consumers, assuming that all ResourceSlices in a pool are updated to
235:	// detect pools which are comprised of multiple ResourceSlices and are
241:	// ResourceSliceCount is the total number of ResourceSlices in the pool at this
245:	// belonging to the same pool.
265:	// the driver in the pool. It must be a DNS label.
305:	// At most one of NodeName, NodeSelector and AllNodes can be set.
312:	// NodeSelector defines the nodes where the device is available.
317:	// At most one of NodeName, NodeSelector and AllNodes can be set.
324:	// AllNodes indicates that all nodes have access to the device.
327:	// At most one of NodeName, NodeSelector and AllNodes can be set.
332:	AllNodes *bool `json:"allNodes,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,7,opt,name=allNodes"`
645:	// nodes is not valid here.
879:	// - All: This request is for all of the matching devices in a pool.
1009:	// - All: This subrequest is for all of the matching devices in a pool.

1457:	// +k8s:listMapKey=pool
1568:	// identify which device was allocated (`<driver name>/<pool name>/<device name>`).

899:		logger.V(4).Info("Clearing health cache for driver upon stream exit", "pluginName", pluginName)

902:			logger.Error(err, "Failed to clear health info cache for driver", "pluginName", pluginName)
911:				logger.V(4).Info("Stopping health monitoring due to context cancellation", "pluginName", pluginName, "reason", err)
916:				logger.V(4).Info("Stream ended with EOF", "pluginName", pluginName)
920:			logger.Error(err, "Error receiving from WatchResources stream", "pluginName", pluginName)

arguments.description: Search for AllocationMode="All" multi-node pool restriction
arguments.prompt:
Search the codebase for where AllocationMode="All" (or DeviceAllocationModeAll) is restricted to single-node pools. Look for:
1. Validation rules that check if AllocationMode=All is used with multi-node pools
2. Comments or documentation explaining the restriction
3. Test cases that verify this restriction
4. Code in allocators that enforces this restriction
5. Any scheduler logic that handles this differently

Focus on:
- /workspace/pkg/apis/resource/validation/ - validation rules
- /workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/structured/internal/ - allocator implementations
- /workspace/pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/dynamicresources/ - scheduler logic

Search for patterns like:
- "single.*node" or "single-node"
- "multi.*node" or "multi-node" 
- "NodeName" combined with "AllocationMode"
- "AllocationMode.*All" combined with restrictions
- Any error messages about pool type and AllocationMode

results.content:
{"type": "text", "text": "Perfect! Now I have enough context. Let me compile my comprehensive report:

Based on my comprehensive search of the codebase, here is what I found regarding restrictions on `AllocationMode="All"` (or `DeviceAllocationModeAll`) in relation to pool types:

## Summary of Findings

The codebase documentation and comments indicate that `AllocationMode="All"` is conceptually designed for single-node pools. The restriction is **documented implicitly through API field comments** but **not explicitly validated** in the code. Here are the key findings:

---

## 1. **API Type Documentation** (Pool Node Selection)
**Location:** `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/api/resource/v1/types.go`

The ResourceSliceSpec defines how pools are distributed across nodes:
- **NodeName**: "identifies the node which provides the resources in this pool" - indicates a **single-node pool**
- **NodeSelector**: "defines which nodes have access to the resources in the pool, **when that pool is not limited to a single node**" - indicates a **multi-node pool**
- **AllNodes**: indicates all nodes have access to the resources in the pool
- **PerDeviceNodeSelection**: per-device node selection

```go
// Line 132: NodeSelector comment
// NodeSelector defines which nodes have access to the resources in the pool,
// when that pool is not limited to a single node.
```

---

## 2. **AllocationMode Documentation**
**Location:** `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/api/resource/v1/types.go` (lines 872-890)

The `ExactDeviceRequest.AllocationMode` documentation states:

```go
// - All: This request is for all of the matching devices in a pool.
//   At least one device must exist on the node for the allocation to succeed.
//   Allocation will fail if some devices are already allocated,
//   unless adminAccess is requested.
```

The phrase "on the node" (singular) suggests the allocation is tied to a **single node**, which aligns with single-node pools (NodeName).

Similarly for `DeviceSubRequest.AllocationMode` (line 1009):
```go
// - All: This subrequest is for all of the matching devices in a pool.
//   Allocation will fail if some devices are already allocated,
//   unless adminAccess is requested.
```

---

## 3. **Validation Rules** 
**Location:** `/workspace/pkg/apis/resource/validation/validation.go` (lines 268-286)

The validation for `AllocationMode` only checks:
```go
func validateDeviceAllocationMode(deviceAllocationMode resource.DeviceAllocationMode, count int64, allocModeFldPath, countFldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {
    switch deviceAllocationMode {
    case resource.DeviceAllocationModeAll:
        if count != 0 {
            allErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(countFldPath, count, 
                fmt.Sprintf("must not be specified when allocationMode is '%s'", deviceAllocationMode)))
        }
    case resource.DeviceAllocationModeExactCount:
        if count <= 0 {
            allErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(countFldPath, count, "must be greater than zero"))
        }
    }
    return allErrs
}
```

**Notably:** There is **NO validation that restricts AllocationMode=All to single-node pools**.

---

## 4. **Allocator Implementation**
**Location:** `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/structured/internal/stable/allocator_stable.go` (lines 402-416)

When processing `AllocationModeAll` requests, the allocator checks:
```go
case resourceapi.DeviceAllocationModeAll:
    // Error checks for pool status
    if pool.IsIncomplete {
        return requestData, fmt.Errorf("claim %s, request %s: asks for all devices, but resource pool %s is currently being updated", ...)
    }
    if pool.IsInvalid {
        return requestData, fmt.Errorf("claim %s, request %s: asks for all devices, but resource pool %s is currently invalid", ...)
    }
```

**Key observation:** The allocator processes `AllocationModeAll` without checking the pool's node selection type (NodeName vs NodeSelector/AllNodes).

---

## 5. **Test Cases**
**Location:** `/workspace/pkg/apis/resource/validation/validation_resourceclaim_test.go`

Test case "allocation-mode" (line ~428) validates:
- AllocationModeAll with Count=0 (valid)
- AllocationModeAll with Count>0 (invalid - "must not be specified when allocationMode is 'All'")

**No tests check for restrictions based on pool node selection type.**

---

## 6. **Changelog Reference**
**Location:** `/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.34.md`

```
- Fixed incorrect behavior for AllocationMode: All in ResourceClaim when used in subrequests. 
  ([#131660](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/131660), [@mortent](https://github.com/mortent)) [SIG Node]
```

This indicates ongoing refinement of AllocationMode=All behavior but doesn't mention single-node restrictions.

---

## Conclusion

**There is NO explicit validation rule in the codebase that restricts `AllocationMode="All"` to single-node pools.** 

The restriction exists only **implicitly through the API documentation** which describes AllocationMode=All allocating "all matching devices in a pool" with wording that suggests single-node contexts ("on the node").

**The actual enforcement depends on:**
1. The allocator's logic when processing multi-node pools
2. The driver implementations
3. Runtime behavior rather than compile-time validation

**Files with relevant code:**
- `/workspace/pkg/apis/resource/validation/validation.go` - validation rules (no pool-type check)
- `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/api/resource/v1/types.go` - API documentation (implicit single-node hint)
- `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/structured/internal/stable/allocator_stable.go` - allocator implementation
- `/workspace/pkg/apis/resource/validation/validation_resourceclaim_test.go` - test cases"}

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have enough context. Let me compile my comprehensive report:

Based on my comprehensive search of the codebase, here is what I found regarding restrictions on `AllocationMode="All"` (or `DeviceAllocationModeAll`) in relation to pool types:

## Summary of Findings

The codebase documentation and comments indicate that `AllocationMode="All"` is conceptually designed for single-node pools. The restriction is **documented implicitly through API field comments** but **not explicitly validated** in the code. Here are the key findings:

---

## 1. **API Type Documentation** (Pool Node Selection)
**Location:** `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/api/resource/v1/types.go`

The ResourceSliceSpec defines how pools are distributed across nodes:
- **NodeName**: "identifies the node which provides the resources in this pool" - indicates a **single-node pool**
- **NodeSelector**: "defines which nodes have access to the resources in the pool, **when that pool is not limited to a single node**" - indicates a **multi-node pool**
- **AllNodes**: indicates all nodes have access to the resources in the pool
- **PerDeviceNodeSelection**: per-device node selection

```go
// Line 132: NodeSelector comment
// NodeSelector defines which nodes have access to the resources in the pool,
// when that pool is not limited to a single node.
```

---

## 2. **AllocationMode Documentation**
**Location:** `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/api/resource/v1/types.go` (lines 872-890)

The `ExactDeviceRequest.AllocationMode` documentation states:

```go
// - All: This request is for all of the matching devices in a pool.
//   At least one device must exist on the node for the allocation to succeed.
//   Allocation will fail if some devices are already allocated,
//   unless adminAccess is requested.
```

The phrase "on the node" (singular) suggests the allocation is tied to a **single node**, which aligns with single-node pools (NodeName).

Similarly for `DeviceSubRequest.AllocationMode` (line 1009):
```go
// - All: This subrequest is for all of the matching devices in a pool.
//   Allocation will fail if some devices are already allocated,
//   unless adminAccess is requested.
```

---

## 3. **Validation Rules** 
**Location:** `/workspace/pkg/apis/resource/validation/validation.go` (lines 268-286)

The validation for `AllocationMode` only checks:
```go
func validateDeviceAllocationMode(deviceAllocationMode resource.DeviceAllocationMode, count int64, allocModeFldPath, countFldPath *field.Path) field.ErrorList {
    switch deviceAllocationMode {
    case resource.DeviceAllocationModeAll:
        if count != 0 {
            allErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(countFldPath, count, 
                fmt.Sprintf("must not be specified when allocationMode is '%s'", deviceAllocationMode)))
        }
    case resource.DeviceAllocationModeExactCount:
        if count <= 0 {
            allErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(countFldPath, count, "must be greater than zero"))
        }
    }
    return allErrs
}
```

**Notably:** There is **NO validation that restricts AllocationMode=All to single-node pools**.

---

## 4. **Allocator Implementation**
**Location:** `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/structured/internal/stable/allocator_stable.go` (lines 402-416)

When processing `AllocationModeAll` requests, the allocator checks:
```go
case resourceapi.DeviceAllocationModeAll:
    // Error checks for pool status
    if pool.IsIncomplete {
        return requestData, fmt.Errorf("claim %s, request %s: asks for all devices, but resource pool %s is currently being updated", ...)
    }
    if pool.IsInvalid {
        return requestData, fmt.Errorf("claim %s, request %s: asks for all devices, but resource pool %s is currently invalid", ...)
    }
```

**Key observation:** The allocator processes `AllocationModeAll` without checking the pool's node selection type (NodeName vs NodeSelector/AllNodes).

---

## 5. **Test Cases**
**Location:** `/workspace/pkg/apis/resource/validation/validation_resourceclaim_test.go`

Test case "allocation-mode" (line ~428) validates:
- AllocationModeAll with Count=0 (valid)
- AllocationModeAll with Count>0 (invalid - "must not be specified when allocationMode is 'All'")

**No tests check for restrictions based on pool node selection type.**

---

## 6. **Changelog Reference**
**Location:** `/workspace/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.34.md`

```
- Fixed incorrect behavior for AllocationMode: All in ResourceClaim when used in subrequests. 
  ([#131660](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/131660), [@mortent](https://github.com/mortent)) [SIG Node]
```

This indicates ongoing refinement of AllocationMode=All behavior but doesn't mention single-node restrictions.

---

## Conclusion

**There is NO explicit validation rule in the codebase that restricts `AllocationMode="All"` to single-node pools.** 

The restriction exists only **implicitly through the API documentation** which describes AllocationMode=All allocating "all matching devices in a pool" with wording that suggests single-node contexts ("on the node").

**The actual enforcement depends on:**
1. The allocator's logic when processing multi-node pools
2. The driver implementations
3. Runtime behavior rather than compile-time validation

**Files with relevant code:**
- `/workspace/pkg/apis/resource/validation/validation.go` - validation rules (no pool-type check)
- `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/api/resource/v1/types.go` - API documentation (implicit single-node hint)
- `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/dynamic-resource-allocation/structured/internal/stable/allocator_stable.go` - allocator implementation
- `/workspace/pkg/apis/resource/validation/validation_resourceclaim_test.go` - test cases
